fix: preserve 'available' key in room_mic._callback state

_callback was replacing self._state with a dict missing the 'available'
key. After the first callback fired, get() returned a dict without
'available', so callers defaulted to True even if the mic had
disconnected mid-session.

- Add 'available': True to the callback's state assignment
- Remove dead self._available instance variable (never read externally)

commit.message:
Fix ESLint: move toolsWereUsed declaration before try block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix staging review tab split and progress counts

Root cause: get_staging_status always generated a presigned thumb_url
for every non-error photo (generate_presigned_url never checks S3), so
photos still processing appeared in "Accept ChromaHaus" with broken
images, and "In Progress" showed 0.

Fix:
- optimize Lambda now writes status="ready" (was "pending_review") when
  processing completes and thumb is written to S3
- get_staging_status only generates thumb_url for status="ready" photos
  (guaranteed to exist); for legacy "pending_review" entries it does a
  head_object check first; error/analyzed get thumb_url=None
- Frontend adds _stagingQueuedCount (photos uploaded but not yet in
  DynamoDB) to "In Progress" tab count, reset by stopStagingPolling
- stagingSelectAll/trashRejected now include status="ready" photos
- 5 new TestStagingStatus tests covering ready/pending/legacy/error/mixed
- All 76 tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add EITC comparison and Bartik context under headline metrics

Adds a 'Scale in context' paragraph after the headline estimates:
- Compares gross cost to EITC (~$70-75B), highlighting that the EITC
  targets household income while the 80-80 Rule targets the employer
  wage rate, with implications for household-composition targeting and
  coverage of single workers and secondary earners
- Cites Bartik (2020) on $80B/yr in government job-creation spending
  concentrated in large-business tax breaks, with limited effectiveness
  in distressed communities where job-finding lags most severely

commit.message:
Donut interactif : 3e handle + snap 1% + rotation offset

Ajout du handle h0 (frontière SCPI/tréso) avec offset local pour
rotation naturelle du donut. Tracking delta frame-par-frame pour
éviter les sauts au wrapping circulaire. Snap passé de 5% à 1%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add structured logging, proactive session refresh, and Cobra CLI

Replace hand-rolled os.Args dispatch with github.com/spf13/cobra for
typed flags, auto-generated help, and built-in shell completion.
Add a 4-level structured logger (Debug/Info/Warn/Error) to stderr and
migrate all diagnostic output from scattered fmt.Fprintf calls.
Add proactive session refresh that re-authenticates when the session
is older than 5 minutes, avoiding wasted failed requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add observed floor safety guard + clarify dashboard labels

- Add _fetch_observed_high_so_far() and _apply_observed_floor() as
  post-prediction safety guards: if it's already 67°F, zero out
  impossible ML buckets below that floor before displaying. This
  prevents embarrassing STRONG_BET signals on already-impossible
  buckets. Applied only to today's predictions (not tomorrow).
- Rename ensemble "Kalshi:" labels to "Bucket: (ensemble est.)" to
  distinguish Gaussian CDF estimates from actual ML predictions
- Add "Atmospheric ML" badge + "v2 classifier | 3+ yrs data" to ML
  Bucket Pick cards so users know the model uses multi-year training

commit.message:
Fix backend JWKS URL: update Clerk domain from guardianos.org to impera.network

Production JWT verification was failing (401 on all authed requests) because
auth.py hardcoded the JWKS URL to clerk.guardianos.org for pk_live_ keys.
Frontend tokens are now issued by the impera.network Clerk instance, so the
backend must fetch verification keys from clerk.impera.network.

into separate V1/V2 paths reading the correct functions for each version.

commit.message:
fix: use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost in server healthchecks

Alpine's wget resolves localhost to ::1 (IPv6) first, but Fastify
bound to 0.0.0.0 only listens on IPv4. This caused healthchecks to
fail despite the server running correctly.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 2-3 signal architecture — rescue redesign + OVER quality scoring

Phase 2 (Rescue Architecture):
- P4: rescue_cap priority sorting — RESCUE_SIGNAL_PRIORITY map sorts by
  validated BB HR weight, not edge. HSE (3) > sharp_book (2) > combo (1).
  Prevents dropping high-HR rescues in favor of low-HR ones.
- P5: Dynamic rescue health gate — RESCUE_MIN_HR_7D=60.0, reads
  signal_health_daily at runtime. Signals below 60% 7d HR automatically
  lose rescue eligibility. Fail-open when health data unavailable.
- P6: combo_he_ms removed from OVER rescue — 25% HR at edge <4, 53.8%
  overall. Kept for UNDER rescue where it performs well.

Phase 3 (OVER Quality Scoring):
- P7: OVER_SIGNAL_WEIGHTS with validated weights. OVER composite =
  edge + quality * 0.3. Signal-rich picks rank higher than signal-poor
  at similar edge. Shadow signals get zero weight.
- P8: Bias-regime OVER volume gate (observation mode) — tracks what
  would block when >70% predictions are UNDER.

Volume tier analysis: filtered edge 4+ picks = 67.6% HR (25-12,
+10.7 units). System leaving profit on table. Key filters destroying
value: over_edge_floor (87.5% CF HR), line_jumped_under (100% CF HR).

80 aggregator tests pass (10 new tests for P4-P7).

commit.message:
Replace billboard toggle with always-on glow sprite (Star.js pattern)

Instead of toggling between flare disc and a billboard at distance,
use an always-visible glow sprite that scales via updateGlow(camera).
This matches how Star.js handles distance visibility — the glow is
hidden behind the bright flare up close but becomes the sole visible
element at distance.

fix: defer background loop thread starts until after dep injection

All background loop threads were being started before
init_background_loops() injected their dependencies, causing
NameError on intent_mgr, plugin, shutdown_event etc. Now threads
are collected in _deferred_threads and started only after
init_background_loops() completes.

commit.message:
fix(relay): stream-json parsing, operator invite, max thread brains limit

- Fix stream-json assistant event parsing: use event.message.content[]
  instead of event.content (string) — previous code never matched assistant
  turns, falling back to result-only posting
- Fix botJoinRoom invite: use operator account (not intercom) to avoid
  M_FORBIDDEN — intercom accounts are write-only broadcast channels that
  are not room members and cannot send invites
- Add MAX_THREAD_BRAINS_PER_BOT=3 (configurable) limit per bot; reject
  new thread_brain IPC tasks when limit reached; track via activeThreadBrainCount
- Add resolveOperatorConfig() helper alongside resolveOperatorUserId()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use dedicated socket in _meta_loop to prevent UDP packet stealing

_run blocks on self._sock.recvfrom for meter blobs; _meta_loop was
also calling recvfrom on the same socket, causing meters and query
responses to be silently swallowed by the wrong thread.

Consolidate _query_float/_query_str/_query_int into a single _query
helper that takes an explicit socket argument. _read_channel_meta now
opens an ephemeral UDP socket for all send/recv during the meta cycle
and closes it in a finally block. self._sock is now exclusively owned
by _run for meter subscriptions.

commit.message:
feat: 5 Jnanendriyas — real active senses implementing SenseProtocol

Steward now perceives its environment deterministically (zero LLM)
through 5 knowledge-acquiring senses from Kapila's Sankhya:

  SROTRA (Ear)    → GitSense      — hears git state
  TVAK   (Skin)   → ProjectSense  — feels project structure
  CAKSU  (Eye)    → CodeSense     — sees code via AST
  JIHVA  (Tongue) → TestSense     — tastes test quality
  GHRANA (Nose)    → HealthSense   — smells code entropy

SenseCoordinator implements ManasProtocol from steward-protocol,
producing AggregatePerception with intensity/quality/pain for Buddhi.

All senses are protocol-based (SenseProtocol), not concrete classes.
Kshetra mappings updated from aliases to real implementations.
45 new tests (720 total). All green.
